symptoms.symptom_name: Acute transient confusion / disorientation (did not recognise carer ~10 min)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute focal neuro disturbance requires exclusion of cerebrovascular cause.
Acute confusion in an immunocompromised patient is delirium until proven otherwise.
CICI can spike acutely under stress/fatigue, but only after mimics excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Transient dysphasia (halting, word-finding failure) during the episode

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Focal speech disturbance is a FAST-positive feature; resolved dysphasia may be a TIA.
Word-finding difficulty is described in CICI but is far less specific than for focal cerebrovascular disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Two-month background of fluctuating short-term memory decline and difficulty multitasking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A subacute fluctuating cognitive decline on chemotherapy is the characteristic CICI pattern.
A gradual 2-month background is not a vascular pattern, though it does not exclude a superimposed acute event.
CICI requires exposure to chemotherapy — an essential contextual risk factor.
Immunosuppression markedly raises the risk of occult infection and delirium.
Active malignancy is the prerequisite risk for intracranial spread.

symptoms.symptom_name: Executive/safety failure — left gas stove running and wandered

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Executive dysfunction is characteristic of CICI, but the acute severity mandates exclusion of organic causes and immediate home-safety action.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever measured on home thermometer (36.8)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A single normal home temperature lowers but does NOT exclude infection in a neutropenic host; confusion can precede fever.

symptoms.symptom_name: No head injury, no sudden severe headache, no vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of headache/vomiting reduces but does not exclude raised intracranial pressure or intracranial disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carer reports no facial droop or one-sided weakness; speech recovered

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A carer's untrained impression is reassurance of low quality — it cannot substitute for a neurological examination and does not exclude stroke/TIA.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neurological examination — cranial nerves II–XII intact, no focal motor/sensory deficit, symmetrical reflexes, normal finger-to-nose

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal focal neuro exam lowers stroke probability — but this is a SEALED clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain or rely upon.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved attention with delayed processing speed on formal assessment (no delirium features)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact attention argues against delirium — a sealed clinician assessment, not available via chat.
Slowed processing with preserved attention fits CICI — but is clinician-assessed, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Brain imaging (CT/MRI) to exclude stroke, haemorrhage or CNS metastasis

investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed at the encounter; planned. Would be required to exclude structural/vascular causes.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A stroke, haemorrhage or intracranial metastasis could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is required to exclude intracranial disease — unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods — full blood count/neutrophils, electrolytes, corrected calcium, glucose

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count and metabolic panel
investigation_details.expected_result: Not performed; required to exclude neutropenia/sepsis and metabolic causes of confusion.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Occult neutropenic sepsis or a metabolic emergency (e.g. hypercalcaemia) could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neutrophil count and cultures are required to exclude febrile/afebrile neutropenic sepsis — unavailable via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden transient dysphasia plus disorientation is a FAST-relevant pattern that mandates urgent exclusion of stroke/TIA before any benign label is accepted.
A fluctuating 2-month cognitive/executive decline on active chemotherapy is the characteristic CICI background — supportive but not confirmatory without exclusion of organic causes.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important step the AI cannot do — required to characterise the episode and exclude focal deficit. Route to a service that can perform it today.
Exclude acute stroke, haemorrhage and CNS metastasis/leptomeningeal disease.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count / neutrophil count, electrolytes, corrected calcium, glucose, +/- blood cultures
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Exclude neutropenic sepsis and metabolic causes of acute confusion in a chemotherapy patient.
Documents cognitive-domain deficits to characterise CICI AFTER organic causes are excluded — not an acute test and not to be prioritised over urgent exclusion of mimics.
